title.zh: 腸道幹細胞革命：顛覆傳統的再生密碼
title.en: Gut Check: How Intestinal Stem Cells Are Rewriting the Rules of Regeneration

summary.zh:
小腸上皮是人體更新最快的組織，每1-2周就會完全更換一次。這個精密系統包含12種功能各異的細胞，從營養吸收到代謝調控各司其職。荷蘭幹細胞權威Hans Clevers的最新綜述揭示：腸道幹細胞的行為完全顛覆傳統認知——它們並非緩慢分裂的『儲備軍』，而是持續對稱分裂的『永動機』。

更驚人的是，這些幹細胞在體外培養中能無限增殖，其分化後的子細胞還具有『逆分化』成幹細胞的驚人可塑性。這項發現不僅改寫組織幹細胞生物學教科書，更為再生醫學開闢新途徑。研究團隊利用小鼠模型和類器官技術，首次完整繪製出隱窩-絨毛結構的分子調控藍圖，這項突破將加速腸道疾病治療的研發進程。
summary.en:
The small intestinal epithelium, with its rapid 1-2 week turnover rate, serves as a paradigm for studying tissue regeneration. Hans Clevers' groundbreaking review in Cell challenges decades-old assumptions about stem cell biology. Contrary to traditional models, intestinal stem cells divide continuously and symmetrically, behaving more like perpetual motion machines than reserved guardians of tissue repair.

Using transgenic mice and revolutionary organoid technology, the research reveals two transformative insights: these stem cells can expand indefinitely in vitro, and their daughter cells display remarkable plasticity to revert back to stem cells during regeneration. This dual discovery not only redefines our understanding of tissue homeostasis but also opens new avenues for treating intestinal disorders. The study provides the first comprehensive molecular map of crypt-villus regulation, offering a roadmap for future regenerative therapies that could benefit millions with gastrointestinal diseases.

keyArticle.title: Shifting paradigms in tissue stem cell biology: Insights from the intestine
